_comment: FireAlive FIDO2 attestation trust anchors. Provenance-pinned, version-controlled, BUNDLED seed of trusted FIDO-certified vendor attestation root CAs. Login enrollment requires a hardware passkey whose attestation chains to one of these roots (or an admin-added root). FireAlive makes no outbound calls: this file is refreshed only by shipping a new release, never fetched at runtime (same constraint as the GeoIP database and bundled AI models). Do not hand-edit PEMs; each was validated as a self-signed X.509 CA certificate before bundling. The seeder (server/db/seed-fido-roots.js) reads the roots array.

_meta.provenancePolicy: Official first-party vendor publications and the FIDO Alliance Metadata Service (MDS, mds.fidoalliance.org) ONLY. No community mirrors, certificate aggregators, blogs, or third-party repositories are ever a source for a bundled trust anchor.
_meta.validation: Every bundled rootPem was parsed with Node crypto.X509Certificate, confirmed self-signed (subject == issuer, self-signature verifies) and a CA certificate, and its DER SHA-256 recorded in sha256Der below.
_meta.refreshModel: Bundled and version-controlled. A root is added or rotated only by shipping a new FireAlive release. There is no runtime fetch from the FIDO MDS and no relax-below-pinning toggle.
_meta.aaguidNarrowing: Optional and OFF by default (empty allow-list = any model from a trusted vendor). Per-vendor aaguids here are left empty; an admin who wants model-level narrowing manages the AAGUID allow-list in the IAM admin surface using the authoritative AAGUID-to-model mapping published by the vendor or the FIDO MDS.

recognizedVendorsForAdminAdd.note: FIDO Alliance Board member. Feitian distributes its FIDO2 attestation roots via the FIDO Alliance MDS (vendor-submitted), not as a standalone PEM on ftsafe.com. Add the MDS-published root via the Trusted Roots admin UI.
Google distributes Titan Security Key attestation roots via the FIDO Alliance MDS. Add the MDS-published root via the Trusted Roots admin UI.

Open-source vendor. Distributes its FIDO2 attestation root via the FIDO Alliance MDS and its official channels (nitrokey.com / github.com/Nitrokey). Add the official root via the Trusted Roots admin UI.

Open-source vendor. Distributes its attestation root via the FIDO Alliance MDS and its official channels (github.com/solokeys). Note that some open hacker/dev editions ship a publicly-known attestation key and therefore do not prove tamper-evident hardware; vet the model before adding its root.

roots.label: Yubico U2F Root CA Serial 457200631
roots.notes: Yubico FIDO/U2F attestation root. Signs FIDO2 / U2F device attestations for YubiKeys with firmware prior to 5.7.4. Published in the OpenPGP-signed Yubico PKI CA bundle.

roots.label: Yubico FIDO Root CA Serial 450203556
roots.notes: Yubico FIDO attestation root. Signs FIDO2 device attestations for newer YubiKeys (firmware 5.7.4 and later). Published in the OpenPGP-signed Yubico PKI CA bundle.
